name: PaperQueue
subtitle: A focused reading queue for your Zotero library.
description: AltStore source for PaperQueue — a native reading-queue companion for your Zotero library.

apps.name: PaperQueue
apps.subtitle: Read more of your Zotero library, with less friction.
apps.localizedDescription:
PaperQueue turns your Zotero library into a focused, curated reading queue. Build a queue, mark papers as read, reorder by priority, search and add by DOI, and browse collections — all synced through Zotero tags so it works across your devices. On iPhone/iPad it's a companion for managing your queue; read the PDFs on your Mac in Zotero.

This build is unsigned — install it with AltStore/SideStore, which re-signs it with your own Apple ID.

versions.localizedDescription:
• macOS: drag-to-reorder in the Queue now works reliably — the double-click-to-open gesture no longer swallows the reorder drag, so you can grab any row and drag it to a new position.
• macOS: the Stats screen now lays its metric cards out as a clean grid of square tiles (icon, value and label centred) instead of stretched-out rectangles.

• Pages read — Stats now estimates pages from Zotero page ranges (e.g. 134–136 = 2, abbreviated 134–36 too): pages today / this week, total pages, a pages-per-week chart, and averages for both works and pages per active day.
• macOS: fixed drag-to-reorder in the Queue (a tap gesture was swallowing the drag), and the right-click menu now offers every action — mark read, read later, skip, remove, open, move to position and move to queue — alongside the visible buttons.

• Authors and editors are both shown now — an edited book or chapter that lists editors no longer hides the actual author. Editors are labelled (e.g. "ed."), and you can filter by either.
• New on Mac: Settings → Updates → "Check for Updates" downloads the latest version from GitHub, replaces the app and relaunches it automatically (falls back to opening the disk image if it can't).

More macOS polish and fixes:
• Library filters and Collections now open at a proper size — the author/tag/year lists and collection contents load instead of appearing empty.
• Settings no longer looks cramped; it uses a clean grouped layout.
• Clicking a paper selects it instantly — the previous selection lag is gone (double-click still opens it).
• The Library now shows the sync progress bar while refreshing, like the Queue.
• Bigger, hover-highlighted row action buttons with better spacing.

Bug fix: on macOS, the "Tags on read" picker in Settings opened empty — your Zotero tags wouldn't load. The picker sheet now sizes correctly, so the full tag list (searchable, multi-select) shows and the tags you choose are applied when you mark a paper read.

A much better Mac experience:
• Single click now just selects a paper; double-click opens it (no more accidental navigation).
• The reading queue shows large, always-visible action buttons on the right of each row — mark read, read later, skip and remove — so you no longer need fiddly swipes.
• History rows get visible buttons too (send back to queue, remove).
• The selected row is clearly highlighted.
These changes are Mac-only; iPhone and iPad keep their swipe gestures.

More control over your reading queue:
• Reorder by number — tap a paper's position badge (or use its context menu) and type the exact spot you want it in the queue.
• Position badges — every paper in the queue now shows its number under the icon.
• Scroll-to-top button — a floating button appears in the Queue, Library and History to jump straight back to the top of long lists.
• Custom tags on read — in Settings, pick one or more Zotero tags (searchable, with the option to add a new one) to apply automatically whenever you mark a paper read, on top of PaperQueue's own tag.

Gamification to keep you reading:
• Daily goal — set how many papers you want to read each day and watch a progress ring fill as you go.
• Streaks — current and best streaks reward consecutive days you hit your goal.
• Reading calendar — every day is coloured green when you meet your goal, orange for partial, red when missed; browse month by month.
• Richer Stats — read today / this week / this month, best day, daily average, plus a weekly chart with your goal line.
• Daily reminder notification at a time you choose, so you never break a streak.
• The widget now shows your streak and today's goal progress.
• Full-library sync fix — standalone PDF/attachment items are no longer skipped, so every item in your Zotero library appears.

• Full bibliography — PaperQueue now loads every item type in your Zotero library (books, book chapters, theses, reports, conference papers…), not just journal articles.
• Much faster sync — the library is fetched with parallel paging, so large libraries load far quicker.
• Sort the Library — order by recently added, oldest added, title, author or publication year, and your choice is remembered.
• Subtler, smoother animations throughout.

• Multiple reading queues — create personalized queues and switch between them (the Add button targets the Default queue unless you pick one).
• Library filters for author, tag and publication year, each with its own search bar, plus the existing status/collection filters.
• Queued papers now show a green check in the Library.
• A progress bar while your library is fetched.
• Cross-device sync for Mac local mode: add a Zotero API key under Settings → Cross-device sync so a Mac reading locally stays in sync with your iPhone/iPad.
Fixed false-positive "You appear to be offline" on pull-to-refresh, improved first-sync responsiveness with a loading state, and added pull-to-refresh to the Library tab.
